volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Casina (Section 3.5.677b - Section 3.6.737b)

sections.label: Section 3.5.677b
sections.translation: For what reason ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.677c
sections.translation: Because you have given her as a wife to Olympio ; she says that she ’ ll neither suffer your life , nor her own , nor that of her husband ,

sections.label: Section 3.5.679_680
sections.original: crástinum protólli : id huc míssa sum tibi ut dícerem ,
sections.translation: to be prolonged until the morrow . I have been sent hither to tell you this , that you might beware of her .

sections.label: Section 3.5.681b
sections.translation: By my troth , to my misery I ’ m quite undone !

sections.label: Section 3.5.682
sections.original: Neque ést neque fuít me senéx quisquam amátor
sections.translation: There neither is nor ever was any old man in love so wretched as I .

sections.label: Section 3.5.683_685b
sections.translation: Don ’ t I play him off cleverly ? For everything that I ’ ve been telling him as taking place , I ’ ve been telling him falsely . My mistress and she who lives next door have concocted this scheme . I ’ ve been sent to fool him .

sections.label: Section 3.5.688b
sections.translation: Hark you , Pardalisca !

sections.label: Section 3.5.689
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.689b
sections.translation: There is —

sections.label: Section 3.5.689c
sections.translation: What ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.689d
sections.original: Ést quod volo éxquirere éx te .
sections.translation: There is something that I want to enquire of you about .

sections.label: Section 3.5.690
sections.translation: You are causing me delay .

sections.label: Section 3.5.690b
sections.translation: Why , you are causing me sorrow . But has Casina got that sword even still ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.692
sections.translation: She has ; but two of them .

sections.label: Section 3.5.692b
sections.translation: Why two ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.692c
sections.translation: She says that this very day she ’ ll murder you with the one , the bailiff with the other .

sections.label: Section 3.5.694
sections.translation: I am now the most utterly murdered of all people that do exist .

sections.label: Section 3.5.695
sections.original: lorícam induám mi optumum ésse opinor .
sections.translation: I ’ ll put on me a coat of mail ; I think that ’ s the best . What did my wife do ? Didn ’ t she go and take them away from her ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.697
sections.translation: No person dares go near her .

sections.label: Section 3.5.697b
sections.translation: She should have prevailed on her .

sections.label: Section 3.5.697c
sections.translation: She is entreating her . She declares that assuredly she will lay them down on no other terms , unless she understands that she shall not be given to the bailiff .

sections.label: Section 3.5.700
sections.original: Atque íngratiís , quia non vólt , nubet hódie .
sections.translation: But whether she likes it or no , because she refuses , she shall marry him this day . For why shouldn ’ t I carry this out that I ’ ve begun , for her to marry me ?— that , indeed , I didn ’ t intend to say — but , our bailiff ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.703b
sections.translation: You ’ re making your mistakes pretty often .

sections.label: Section 3.5.704
sections.original: Timór praepedít verba . vérum , obsecró te ,
sections.translation: It ’ s alarm that impedes my words . But , prithee ,

sections.label: Section 3.5.705
sections.original: dic méd uxorem órare ut éxoret íllam ,
sections.translation: do tell my wife , that I entreat her to prevail upon her to put down the sword , and allow me to return in-doors .

sections.label: Section 3.5.706b
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell her .

sections.label: Section 3.5.707
sections.translation: And do you entreat her .

sections.label: Section 3.5.707b
sections.translation: And I ’ ll entreat her .

sections.label: Section 3.5.707c
sections.original: At blande órato , ut solés . sed audin ?
sections.translation: And in soft language , in your usual way . But do you hear me ? If you manage this , I ’ ll give you a pair of shoes [*] and a gold ring [*] for your finger , and plenty of nice things .

sections.label: Section 3.5.710
sections.translation: I ’ ll do my best .

sections.label: Section 3.5.710b
sections.translation: Take care and prevail .

sections.label: Section 3.5.711
sections.translation: Now then I ’ ll be off ; unless you detain me for anything .

sections.label: Section 3.5.712b
sections.translation: Be off , and take care .

sections.label: Section 3.5.713_719
sections.original: redit éccum tandem opsónatu meus ádiutor , pompám ducit .
sections.translation: Look , his assistant is returning , at last , with the provisions ; he ’ s bringing a train after him . ( She goes into the house .)

sections.label: Section 3.6.720
sections.original: Vide , fúr , sentis súb signis ducás .
sections.translation: See , you thief , that you lead on your briars beneath their banners [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.6.720b
sections.translation: But how are they briars ?

sections.label: Section 3.6.721
sections.original: Quia quód tetigere , ilíco rapiunt , si eás ereptum , ilíco scindunt :
sections.translation: Because that which they have touched , they instantly seize hold of ; if you go to snatch it from them , they instantly rend it ; so , wherever they come , wherever they are , with a twofold loss [*] do they mulct their masters .

sections.label: Section 3.6.723
sections.translation: Heyday , indeed !

sections.label: Section 3.6.723b
sections.original: Áttat . cesso mágnufice patriciéque amicirier átque ita ero meo ire ádvorsum ?
sections.translation: Well , well ! This way I ’ m delaying to go meet my master with a magnificent , patrician , and patronizing air . ( He struts along .)

sections.label: Section 3.6.724_725
sections.translation: My good man , save you .

sections.label: Section 3.6.724_725b
sections.translation: I admit that so I am [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.6.724_725c
sections.translation: How goes it ?

sections.label: Section 3.6.724_725d
sections.original: Tu amás : ego ésurio et sitio .
sections.translation: You are in love , but I ’ m hungering and thirsting .

sections.label: Section 3.6.726
sections.translation: You have come capitally provided .

sections.label: Section 3.6.726b
sections.translation: Pooh ! pooh !

sections.label: Section 3.6.727
sections.translation: But stop you , although you do hold me in contempt —

sections.label: Section 3.6.727b
sections.original: Fu fú , fetet tuos míhi sermo .
sections.translation: O dear , O dear ! your converse has a bad smell to me . ( Moving away .)

sections.label: Section 3.6.728
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 3.6.728b
sections.original: Haec res . etiámne adstas ? enim véro πράγματά μοι παρέχεις .
sections.translation: That ’ s the matter .

sections.label: Section 3.6.729
sections.translation: I shall be giving you a grand coup [*] , I fancy , if you don ’ t stand still forthwith . ( Catches hold of him .)

sections.label: Section 3.6.730b
sections.translation: O mon Dieu [*] ! Can ’ t you get away from me , unless you would like me to be sick just now ?

sections.label: Section 3.6.732
sections.translation: Do stop a bit .

sections.label: Section 3.6.732b
sections.original: Quid est ? quis hic ést homo ?
sections.translation: How ’ s this ? ( Staring at him .) What person ’ s this ?

sections.label: Section 3.6.733_735
sections.translation: I ’ m your master .

sections.label: Section 3.6.733_735b
sections.translation: What master ?

sections.label: Section 3.6.733_735c
sections.translation: He whose slave you are .

sections.label: Section 3.6.733_735d
sections.translation: I , a slave ?

sections.label: Section 3.6.733_735e
sections.translation: Aye , and mine .

sections.label: Section 3.6.736
sections.translation: Am I not a free man ? Remember ! remember !

sections.label: Section 3.6.737b
sections.translation: Stop and stay you there !
